caseDescription: The vermilion dispatch seal for Higashi Loop Operations Annex vanished after an overnight timetable audit, and a reroute envelope disappeared with it. Five staff members remained inside the mezzanine control wing, but only one suspect survives the logic of the true clue, the planted red herring, and the neutral timing detail.
locationImagePromptBase: empty rail operations mezzanine environment plate after service shutdown, unoccupied central floor area, polished tile floor with liquid reflections, blank display panels, dark monitor glass, erased floor graphics, abstract luminous bars instead of signs, glass control partitions, platform rails and signal glow beyond the windows, layered architectural depth, no people, no characters, no human figures, no silhouettes, no passengers, no staff, no letters, no numbers, no words
featuredImagePromptBase: overnight transit control annex after a theft, empty vermilion seal hook beside an open dispatch cabinet, missing reroute envelope, cool platform light through the glass, no people, physical clue atmosphere
instruction: Study the lineup, compare each interview, and weigh the evidence roles carefully: one clue is true, one clue is a trap, and one only clarifies the timing.
explanation: The guilty suspect is Haruto Shindo, slot 4. The valid evidence is Vermilion Wax Fold, which places Haruto at the dispatch cabinet after the audit because the same vermilion security wax used on the missing reroute envelope is pressed into the inner fold of his dispatch folio. Copper Relay Smear is the red herring: it points toward Kenji Arai because he works daily with copper contact grease on the signal relays, so that transfer comes from routine maintenance rather than the theft. Timetable Dust Gap is neutral evidence; it proves the schedule strip was swapped later than the suspects claimed and narrows the time window, but it does not accuse anyone by itself. Haruto had custody over the reroute paperwork, lied about staying at the dispatch desk, and is the only suspect consistent with the wax transfer, motive, and narrowed timeline.

suspects.dialogue: I stayed on mezzanine sightlines and checked the last platform clear. I did not open the dispatch cabinet, and I never touched the reroute envelope.
suspects.profile: Age 37, disciplined platform supervisor who treats platform order and timing like a public promise she cannot afford to break.
suspects.motive: Could want to hide a safety lapse during the late audit that would damage her spotless station record.
suspects.notes: She had line-of-sight authority but no strong physical link to the cabinet wax or missing envelope.

visualIdentity.silhouette: structured transit blazer over a narrow trouser line
visualIdentity.wardrobe: navy station blazer, pressed slacks, gloves, polished low heels
visualIdentity.hair: dark bob tucked cleanly behind one ear
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: silver whistle tab at the lapel
visualIdentity.props: folded platform clipboard and inspection pen

suspects.portraitPromptBase: platform supervisor, structured transit blazer over a narrow trouser line, polished low heels, silver whistle tab, folded platform clipboard, inspection pen, teal route-stripe accent, upright command stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: If there is copper grease near that cabinet, it came off my maintenance gloves earlier in the shift. I service relay housings; I do not forge reroute orders.
suspects.profile: Age 41, pragmatic signal electrician who keeps the annex alive through methodical relay checks and late-night fixes.
suspects.motive: Could want to conceal an unauthorized relay patch that would trigger a formal maintenance inquiry.
suspects.notes: The red herring points toward him because he works around copper grease constantly, but the real paper trail does not hold.

visualIdentity.silhouette: boxy utility jacket with heavy tool satchel
visualIdentity.wardrobe: maintenance jacket, rolled sleeves, work trousers, heavy lace-up boots
visualIdentity.hair: short black hair with a rough side part
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: orange insulated tester clipped to the chest
visualIdentity.props: coiled wire lead and relay wrench set

suspects.portraitPromptBase: signal electrician, boxy utility jacket with heavy tool satchel, rolled sleeves, heavy lace-up boots, orange insulated tester, coiled wire lead, relay wrench set, amber safety piping, square planted stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I was reconciling fare discrepancies at the side terminal. I logged the late swap, but I never opened the dispatch cabinet or handled the seal.
suspects.profile: Age 29, careful fare audit clerk whose calm precision makes her the one staff member everyone trusts with tedious late-night reconciliations.
suspects.motive: Could want to hide a refund discrepancy that would expose a messy accounting shortcut.
suspects.notes: Her timeline matters because she saw the schedule strip move, but the neutral clue stops at narrowing the window.

visualIdentity.silhouette: long cardigan over a clipped office skirt shape
visualIdentity.wardrobe: soft cardigan, fitted blouse, office skirt, sensible flats
visualIdentity.hair: straight dark hair tied in a low ribboned ponytail
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: thin ID strap with a silver card clip
visualIdentity.props: receipt folio and compact adding machine

suspects.portraitPromptBase: fare audit clerk, long cardigan over a clipped office skirt shape, sensible flats, thin ID strap, receipt folio, compact adding machine, plum notebook band accent, reserved inward stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I stayed at dispatch and signed off the audit from my desk. I had no reason to pocket the seal or touch the cabinet once the paperwork was closed.
suspects.profile: Age 34, controlled night dispatch coordinator who manages route paperwork, staff pressure, and sudden timetable changes without showing strain.
suspects.motive: Needed to remove the reroute envelope before it exposed a forged freight diversion signed under his watch.
suspects.notes: He had direct paper custody, the clearest motive, and the only interview statement cleanly broken by the valid transfer clue.

visualIdentity.silhouette: long dispatch coat over a slim suit line
visualIdentity.wardrobe: dark office coat, narrow tie, fitted trousers, polished shoes
visualIdentity.hair: neatly parted black hair with a precise side sweep
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: vermilion route tab tucked in the breast pocket
visualIdentity.props: structured folio and timetable stamp case

suspects.portraitPromptBase: night dispatch coordinator, long dispatch coat over a slim suit line, polished shoes, vermilion route tab, structured folio, timetable stamp case, deep red binding accent, still guarded stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: My team handled the mezzanine floor after the audit, not the cabinet paperwork. I noticed the schedule strip moved, but I left the seal drawer alone.
suspects.profile: Age 39, direct cleaning contractor lead who knows every corridor, spill, and after-hours habit in the annex better than the salaried staff do.
suspects.motive: Could want to hide unsanctioned subcontract access that happened during the late shift.
suspects.notes: Her access explains how she notices timeline details, but there is no direct chain from her supplies to the stolen seal.

visualIdentity.silhouette: cleaning coat with a strong vertical apron line
visualIdentity.wardrobe: work coat, apron panel, utility trousers, durable sneakers
visualIdentity.hair: dark hair in a high wrapped bun
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: key ring clipped at the belt
visualIdentity.props: folded caution stand and spray bottle holster

suspects.portraitPromptBase: cleaning contractor lead, cleaning coat with a strong vertical apron line, durable sneakers, key ring, folded caution stand, spray bottle holster, lime safety band accent, forward no-nonsense stance, full body standing

evidence.title: Copper Relay Smear
evidence.description: A fresh copper-grease smear on the dispatch cabinet edge appears to match Kenji's maintenance gloves, making him look like the obvious culprit at first glance.
evidence.imagePromptBase: fresh copper relay grease on a cabinet edge beside insulated maintenance gloves and a small relay housing, macro physical clue detail

evidence.title: Vermilion Wax Fold
evidence.description: A pressed fold of vermilion security wax from the missing reroute envelope is caught inside Haruto's dispatch folio.
evidence.imagePromptBase: vermilion security wax pressed into a dark folio fold beside a cabinet scratch and paper fibers, macro physical clue detail

evidence.title: Timetable Dust Gap
evidence.description: A clean gap in the mezzanine timetable dust proves the schedule strip was swapped later than the suspects first claimed, narrowing the theft window.
evidence.imagePromptBase: clean dust gap on a timetable shelf beside shifted route strips and platform lamp residue, contextual operations clue through materials and arrangement
